description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington is a state in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Ohio (  oh-HY-oh) is a state in the Midwestern region of the United States.

aliases: Agricultural College, Experiment Station and School of Science of the State of Washington
description: public university in Pullman, Washington, USA
description_wiki: Washington State University (WSU, or colloquially Wazzu) is a public land-grant research university in Pullman, Washington, United States.

description: American Baptist minister, activist, civil rights leader, politician, and founder of the Rainbow PUSH Coalition (1941–2026)
description_wiki: Jesse Louis Jackson Sr.

description: county in Washington, United States
description_wiki: Spokane County is a county located in the U.S. state of Washington.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: American politician
description_wiki: Lawrence Edward Gossett (born February 21, 1945) is an American politician and activist who served as a member of the nonpartisan King County Council, representing District 10 from 1994 to 2006 and District 2 from 2006 to 2020.

description: public university in Bellingham, Washington
description_wiki: Western Washington University (WWU or Western) is a public university in Bellingham, Washington, United States.

description: city in, and the county seat of, Whatcom County in the State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Bellingham ( BEL-ing-ham) is a city in and the county seat of Whatcom County in the U.S. state of Washington.

description: day in the US presidential election when many states hold primary elections
description_wiki: Super Tuesday is the United States presidential primary election day in February or March when the greatest number of U.S. states hold primary elections and caucuses.

description: county in the state of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Whatcom County (, ) is a county located in the northwestern corner of the U.S. state of Washington, bordered by the Lower Mainland (the Metro Vancouver and Fraser Valley Regional Districts) of the Canadian province of British Columbia to the north, Okanogan County to the east, Skagit County to the south, San Juan County across Rosario Strait to the southwest, and the Strait of Georgia to the west.

description: city in and county seat of Thurston County, Washington, United States, and Washington federated state capital city
description_wiki: Olympia (Lushootseed: st̕əč̓as) is the capital city of the U.S. state of Washington.

description: American politician
description_wiki: Michael Stanley Dukakis ( duu-KAH-kiss; born November 3, 1933) is an American politician and lawyer who was governor of Massachusetts from 1975 to 1979 and from 1983 to 1991.

description: county in the state of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Yakima County is a county in the U.S. state of Washington.

description: seat of Clark County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Vancouver (  van-KOO-vər) is a city in Clark County, Washington, United States, located on the north bank of the Columbia River.

description: mountain range in western North America
description_wiki: The Cascade Range, or Cascades, is a major mountain range of western North America, extending from southern British Columbia through Washington and Oregon to Northern California.

description: Latino activist (1938-2010)
description_wiki: Roberto Maestas (July 9, 1938 – September 22, 2010) was an American social activist who was prominent between the 1960s and the 1990s in Seattle, Washington.

description: public high school in Seattle, USA
description_wiki:
Franklin High School is a public high school in Seattle, Washington,
located in its Mount Baker neighborhood and administered by Seattle Public Schools.

description: movement with the stated goal of achieving Mexican American empowerment
description_wiki: The Chicano Movement, also referred to as El Movimiento (Spanish for "the Movement"), was a social and political movement in the United States that worked to embrace a Chicano identity and worldview that combated structural racism, encouraged cultural revitalization, and achieved community empowerment by rejecting assimilation.

description: native American cultural center in Seattle, WA
description_wiki: The Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center is a Native American cultural center in Seattle, Washington, described by its parent organization United Indians of All Tribes as "an urban base for Native Americans in the Seattle area." Located on 20 acres (81,000 m2) in Seattle's Discovery Park in the Magnolia neighborhood, the center developed from activism by Bernie Whitebear and other Native Americans, who staged a generally successful self-styled "invasion" and occupation of the land in 1970.

aliases: Black Pather Party For Self Defense
description: US Black power organization (1966–1982)
description_wiki: The Black Panther Party (BPP; originally the Black Panther Party for Self-Defense) was an American Marxist–Leninist and black power political and militant organization founded by college students Bobby Seale and Huey P. Newton in October 1966 in Oakland, California and active in that area until 1982.
